title: Guide to Te Marautanga o Aotearoa – Tau 0 to 8 - Ministry of Education
content:
He aratohu mā ngā akoranga o Te Marautanga o Aotearoa i ia tau

## Guide to Te Marautanga o Aotearoa – Tau 0 to 8
The Parent Portal sets out what your mokopuna are learning at kura each year, and how you and your whānau can support them to achieve.
- Guide to Te Marautanga o Aotearoa – Tau 0 to 8

We’re inviting whānau to share their feedback on the Parent Portal to help us improve the information and support we provide. Take a few minutes to complete our short survey.
Parent Portal survey – Survey Monkey

## Understanding what your mokopuna learns #
The Parent Portal is a collection of guidance and resources for whānau.
This year–by–year learning guide covers Te Marautanga o Aotearoa and currently covers Tau 1 to 8. We'll be adding more soon.
The guide is structured by year level. Find the level of your mokopuna. Within that section, you will find ways to understand what they do at kura, and how you can support them to achieve what they need to this year.
- Ono marama – Te Marautanga o Aotearoa Starting kura is a big milestone for mokopuna. Here is some helpful information to support their first 6 months at kura. Find out more

### Ono marama – Te Marautanga o Aotearoa
Starting kura is a big milestone for mokopuna. Here is some helpful information to support their first 6 months at kura.
- Tau 1 – Te Marautanga o Aotearoa Each year mokopuna will be building on their learning. Here is some helpful information to support their learning in Te Reo Rangatira and Pāngarau. Find out more

### Tau 1 – Te Marautanga o Aotearoa
Each year mokopuna will be building on their learning. Here is some helpful information to support their learning in Te Reo Rangatira and Pāngarau.
- Tau 2 – Te Marautanga o Aotearoa Understand what your child will have learned by the end of Tau 2 at kura, and how you can support this learning at home. Find out more

- Tau 8 – Te Marautanga o Aotearoa Understand what your child will have learned by Tau 8 at kura, plus guidance on ways you can help them further their learning at home. Find out more

### Tau 8 – Te Marautanga o Aotearoa
Understand what your child will have learned by Tau 8 at kura, plus guidance on ways you can help them further their learning at home.
There are lots of fun ways that you can support mokopuna learning at home. We’ve provided some ideas and some simple ways that you as a whānau can weave learning into your whānau routine.
Mokopuna will develop their te reo Māori language skills through engaging with hands-on activities and being exposed to reo Māori that builds and expands on their existing knowledge.  They will explore how pānui, tuhituhi and kōrero help them understand the world and communicate their ideas effectively. With the support of their kaiako, they will learn to ask thoughtful questions, interpret texts, and express themselves clearly through writing and discussion.
You can support your mokopuna with their pāngarau learning at home in many fun ways. We’ve provided some ideas and some simple ways that you as a whānau can weave learning into your whānau routine.
Mokopuna will build their pāngarau skills through fun, hands-on activities that build on what they already know. They will discover how pāngarau can help them solve problems and make sense of the world around them. With the support of their kaiako, mokopuna will learn to ask good questions and work through pāngarau activities one step at a time.
